3265	\begin{definition}\leanok
3266	[Schematic-support immersion]
3267	  \label{def:schematic_support_immersion}
3268	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.schematicSupportι}
3269	  \uses{def:schematic_support, def:modules_annihilator}
3270	  Let \(\mathcal{F}\) be a sheaf of modules on a scheme \(X\). The
3271	  \emph{schematic-support immersion} is the canonical closed immersion
3272	  \[
3273	    \mathrm{schematicSupport}(\mathcal{F}) \;\hookrightarrow\; X
3274	  \]
3275	  of the schematic support (\cref{def:schematic_support}) into the ambient
3276	  scheme, namely the subscheme immersion of the annihilator ideal sheaf
3277	  \(\mathrm{Ann}(\mathcal{F})\) (\cref{def:modules_annihilator}). It is a
3278	  quasi-compact preimmersion onto the support, and it is the morphism whose
3279	  composite with a base morphism \(f : X \to S\) defines proper support
3280	  (\cref{def:has_proper_support}). Proved directly in Lean.
3281	\end{definition}

3283	\begin{lemma}[Properness as a morphism property]
3284	  \label{lem:isProper_mathlib}
3285	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.IsProper}
3286	  \mathlibok
3287	  \textit{Provided by Mathlib
3288	  (\texttt{Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Morphisms.Proper}).}
3289	  A morphism \(f : X \to S\) of schemes is \emph{proper} iff it is separated,
3290	  universally closed, and locally of finite type. Properness is stable under base
3291	  change: if \(f : X \to S\) is proper and \(g : T \to S\) is any morphism then
3292	  the base change \(X \times_S T \to T\) is proper. Consequently the base-change
3293	  clause that Nitsure invokes (``properness \ldots\ preserved by base-change'')
3294	  applies directly to the proper-support condition below.
3295	\end{lemma}

3362

3366	  % + L481-L498).
3367	  % SOURCE QUOTE:

3369	  %  scheme over it. Let \(E\) be a coherent sheaf on \(X\).
3370	  %  Let \(Sch_S\) denote the category of all locally noetherian schemes

3382	  %

3384	  %  be regarded as equivalent if \(\ker(q) = \ker(q')\)),
3385	  %  and \(\langle \F, q \rangle\) will denote
3386	  %  an equivalence class. Then as properness and flatness are preserved
3387	  %  by base-change, and as tensor-product is right exact,
3388	  %  the pull-back of \(\langle \F, q \rangle\) under an \(S\)-morphism \(T'\to T\) is
3389	  %  well-defined, which gives
3390	  %  a set-valued contravariant functor
3391	  %  \(\Quot_{E/X/S} : Sch_S \to Sets\) under which
3392	  %  \(\)T\mapsto  \{\mbox{ All } \langle \F, q \rangle \mbox{ parametrised by }T\}\(\)
3393	  %  [...]
3394	  %  This shows that the functor \(\Quot_{E/X/S}\) naturally decomposes
3395	  %  as a co-product
3396	  %  \(\)\Quot_{E/X/S} = \coprod_{{\Phi}\in \Q[\lambda]}\, \Quot_{E/X/S}^{\Phi,L}\(\)
3397	  %  where for any polynomial \({\Phi}\in \Q[\lambda]\), the functor
3398	  %  \(\Quot_{E/X/S}^{\Phi,L}\) associates
3399	  %  to any \(T\) the set of all equivalence classes of
3400	  %  families \(\langle \F,q \rangle\) such that at each \(t\in T\) the
3401	  %  Hilbert polynomial of the restriction \(\F_t\), calculated
3402	  %  using the pull-back of \(L\), is \({\Phi}\)."
3403	  \textit{Source: [Nitsure], \S 1 (FGA Explained Ch.~5).}
3404	  Let \(S\) be a noetherian scheme, \(\pi : X \to S\) a finite-type morphism,
3405	  \(L\) a line bundle on \(X\), \(E\) a coherent sheaf on \(X\), and
3406	  \(\Phi \in \mathbb{Q}[\lambda]\). The \emph{Quot functor with Hilbert
3407	  polynomial \(\Phi\)}
3408	  \[
3409	    \Quot^{\Phi,L}_{E/X/S} : (\mathrm{Sch}/S)^{op} \longrightarrow \mathbf{Set}
3410	  \]
3411	  sends an \(S\)-scheme \(T \to S\) to the set of equivalence classes
3412	  \(\langle \mathcal{F}, q\rangle\) of pairs \((\mathcal{F}, q)\) where
3413	  \begin{enumerate}
3414	    \item \(\mathcal{F}\) is a coherent sheaf on \(X_T = X \times_S T\) whose
3415	      schematic support is proper over \(T\) (\cref{def:has_proper_support}) and
3416	      which is flat over \(T\);
3417	    \item \(q : E_T \twoheadrightarrow \mathcal{F}\) is a surjective
3418	      \(\mathcal{O}_{X_T}\)-linear homomorphism, where \(E_T\) is the pull-back
3419	      of \(E\) along \(X_T \to X\);
3420	    \item for every \(t \in T\) the Hilbert polynomial of \(\mathcal{F}|_{X_t}\)
3421	      with respect to \(L|_{X_t}\) (\cref{def:hilbert_polynomial}) equals
3422	      \(\Phi\).
3423	  \end{enumerate}
3424	  Two pairs \((\mathcal{F}, q)\) and \((\mathcal{F}', q')\) are equivalent iff
3425	  \(\ker(q) = \ker(q')\). On morphisms \(f : T' \to T\) over \(S\), the functor
3426	  acts by pulling back the quotient along \(X_{T'} \to X_T\) (well-defined
3427	  since tensor product is right-exact and preserves both flatness and
3428	  properness). The Hilbert scheme is the special case
3429	  \(\Hilb^{\Phi,L}_{X/S} := \Quot^{\Phi,L}_{\mathcal{O}_X/X/S}\).
3430	\end{definition}

3432	\section{The Grassmannian scheme}
3433	\label{sec:grassmannian_scheme}
3434	
3435	The Quot construction proceeds by embedding \(\Quot^{\Phi,L}_{E/X/S}\) as a
3436	locally closed subfunctor of a Grassmannian over \(S\). Since Mathlib carries
3437	no Grassmannian \emph{scheme} (only a finite-rank-quotient variety in
3438	linear algebra), we package the construction here.
3439	
3440	\begin{definition}\leanok

3448	  % + L773-L850).

3452	  %  \(\grass(r,d)\) over \(\Z\), together with the tautological
3453	  %  quotient \(u:\oplus^r\,{\mathcal O}_{\grass(r,d)} \to \U\) where \
3454	  %  \(\U\) is a rank \(d\) locally free sheaf on \(\grass(r,d)\),
3455	  %  has been given at the end of this section. [...]

3457	  %  \(u:\oplus^r\,{\mathcal O}_{\grass(r,d)} \to \U\) represents the

3459	  %  \(\)\Grass(r,d) = \Quot_{\oplus^r\,{\mathcal O}_{\Z}/\Z/\Z}^{d, {\mathcal O}_{\Z}}\(\)
3460	  %  from schemes to sets, which associates to any \(T\) the
3461	  %  set of all equivalence classes
3462	  %  \(\langle \F, q\rangle\) of quotients \(q: \oplus^r\,{\mathcal O}_T \to \F\)
3463	  %  where \(\F\) is a locally free sheaf on \(T\) of rank \(d\).
3464	  %  [...]
3465	  %  Using the above show that, more generally, if \(S\) is a scheme and
3466	  %  \(E\) is a locally free \({\mathcal O}_S\)-module of rank \(r\),
3467	  %  the functor \(\Grass(E,d)=\Quot_{E/S/S}^{d,{\mathcal O}_S}\)
3468	  %  on all \(S\)-schemes which
3469	  %  by definition associates to any \(T\)
3470	  %  the set of all equivalence classes \(\langle \F, q\rangle\) of quotients
3471	  %  \(q: E_T \to \F\) where \(\F\) is a locally free sheaf on \(T\) of rank \(d\),
3472	  %  is representable. The representing scheme

3476	  Grassmannian" (FGA Explained Ch.~5).}
3477	  Let \(S\) be a noetherian scheme and let \(V\) be a locally free
3478	  \(\mathcal{O}_S\)-module of rank \(r\). For an integer \(1 \le d \le r\), the
3479	  \emph{Grassmannian of rank-\(d\) quotients of \(V\)} is the functor
3480	  \[
3481	    \mathrm{Grass}(V, d) : (\mathrm{Sch}/S)^{op} \longrightarrow \mathbf{Set},
3482	    \qquad
3483	    T \longmapsto \{ \langle \mathcal{F}, q\rangle :

3576	% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: see the "Construction of Grassmannian" sub-section of
3577	% [Nitsure], \S 1 (references/nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex,
3578	% L773-L930), which gives the construction in five named steps
3579	% ("Construction by gluing together affine patches", "Separatedness",
3580	% "Properness", "Universal quotient", "Projective embedding"). The total
3581	% verbatim text is ~150 lines; it is reproduced piecewise in the
3582	% SOURCE QUOTE comment of the theorem statement above.
3583	\begin{proof}
3584	  
3585	  Over \(S = \Spec \mathbb{Z}\) and \(V = \mathcal{O}_S^{\oplus r}\) the
3586	  Grassmannian \(\mathrm{Gr}(r, d)\) is constructed by gluing
3587	  \(\binom{r}{d}\) affine charts \(U^I = \Spec\mathbb{Z}[X^I]\) indexed by
3588	  size-\(d\) subsets \(I \subseteq \{1, \dots, r\}\). The chart \(U^I\)
3589	  carries the matrix \(X^I\) of size \(d \times r\) whose \(I\)-th \(d \times d\)
3590	  minor is the identity, with the remaining \(d(r-d)\) entries free
3591	  variables (so \(U^I \cong \mathbb{A}^{d(r-d)}_{\mathbb{Z}}\)). The
3592	  transition maps \(\theta_{I,J} : U^I \cap U^J \to U^J \cap U^I\) defined
3593	  by \(X^J \mapsto (X^I_J)^{-1} X^I\) on the locus where \(\det X^I_J\) is
3594	  invertible satisfy the cocycle condition
3595	  \(\theta_{I,K} = \theta_{I,J} \circ \theta_{J,K}\). The glued
3596	  \(\mathrm{Gr}(r,d) \to \Spec\mathbb{Z}\) is smooth of relative dimension
3597	  \(d(r-d)\). Separatedness is checked from the diagonal
3598	  \(\Delta_{I,J} \subset U^I \times U^J\) cut out by \(X^J_I X^I - X^J = 0\).
3599	  Properness follows from the valuative criterion for DVRs: given
3600	  \(\varphi : \Spec K \to \mathrm{Gr}(r,d)\) over a DVR \(R\) with fraction
3601	  field \(K\), factoring through some chart \(U^I\) via a ring map
3602	  \(f : \mathbb{Z}[X^I] \to K\), choose \(J\) minimising the valuation
3603	  \(\nu(f(P^I_J))\) of the minor; on the chart \(U^J\) all entries have
3604	  non-negative valuation, so \(f\) extends uniquely to \(\Spec R\).
3605	
3606	  The universal quotient \(u : \mathcal{O}_{\mathrm{Gr}(r,d)}^{\oplus r}
3607	  \twoheadrightarrow \mathcal{U}\) is given on \(U^I\) by the matrix \(X^I\)
3608	  and glued by \(g_{I,J} = (X^I_J)^{-1} \in GL_d(U^I \cap U^J)\). The

## Iter-030 additions: the 6 `OverSiteSheafEquivalence` declarations

This section answers directive question (a): are the 6 new decls honest coverage debt, not fake
statements; were any protected stubs silently re-typed?

**Declarations added (L786–882):**

1. `overEquivalence_functor_isCocontinuous` (instance, L787) — cover-lifting for the functor of
   `Opens.overEquivalence U`. Real proof: constructs the covering sieve using
   `GrothendieckTopology.mem_over_iff`, `Sieve.overEquiv_iff`, and `S.downward_closed`.

2. `overEquivalence_inverse_isCocontinuous` (instance, L815) — cover-lifting for the inverse.
   Real proof: analogous construction using preimage opens.

3. `overEquivalence_inverse_isDenseSubsite` (instance, L841) — derived from the two
   cocontinuities via `Equivalence.isDenseSubsite_inverse_of_isCocontinuous`. Three-line body.

4. `overEquivalence_functor_isContinuous` (instance, L849) — derived from cocontinuity of the
   inverse via the adjunction `Equivalence.symm.toAdjunction.isContinuous_of_isCocontinuous`.

5. `overEquivalence_inverse_isContinuous` (instance, L860) — derived from cocontinuity of the
   functor via the adjunction `Equivalence.toAdjunction.isContinuous_of_isCocontinuous`.

6. `overEquivalence_sheafCongr` (noncomputable def, L877) — the headline result:
   `Sheaf ((grothendieckTopology X).over U) A ≌ Sheaf (grothendieckTopology ↥U) A`,
   assembled by `(Opens.overEquivalence U).sheafCongr`.

**Are they honest coverage debt?**

Yes. The Lean file's section header (L751–775) explicitly cites the Mathlib `Topology/Sheaves/Over.lean`
TODO: "show that both functors of the equivalence `overEquivalence U` are continuous and induce an
equivalence between `Sheaf ((Opens.grothendieckTopology X).over U) A` and
`Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology U) A`". The 6 declarations fill this TODO. They have no blueprint
`\lean{}` pins — exactly as expected for project-local infrastructure supporting the future
`lem:over_restrict_iso` (C).

**Are any protected stubs silently re-typed?**

No. `archon-protected.yaml` lists (as suggested entries, not active entries) only the four
skeleton declarations: `hilbertPolynomial`, `QuotFunctor`, `Grassmannian`,
`Grassmannian.representable`. None of the 6 new declarations appear there or elsewhere as protected.
No existing declaration in the file was renamed or re-typed.

**Body quality:**

All 6 have substantive, non-sorry, non-trivially-true bodies. Declarations 1–2 contain the actual
mathematical work (cover-lifting proofs). Declarations 3–6 are derived combinatorially from 1–2.

## Blueprint adequacy for this file

### (a) Coverage

Counting only declarations in `QuotScheme.lean`:

- **20 declarations have a `\lean{}` pin** in the blueprint (including the 4 sorry skeletons,
  the 2 private helpers, the `\mathlibok`-only anchors, and the full set of non-sorry proofs).
- **6 declarations are unreferenced** (the OverSiteSheafEquivalence infra, all intentional).
- **6 absent `\lean{}` pins** for gap1-cone declarations (C, P1, D, gap1, G1-core, isLocalizedModule):
  all correctly **not** marked `\leanok`; blueprint NOTEs explain absence.

Coverage: adequate for all existing axiom-clean declarations. The 6 new declarations are
legitimately outside the blueprint's current declaration graph.

### (b) Proof-sketch depth for the gap1 cone (C → P1 → D → assemble)

| Step | Blueprint detail | Verdict |
|------|-----------------|---------|
| **C** `lem:over_restrict_iso` | Proof says: "The open immersion `U.ι` induces an equivalence between the slice of the opens site over `U` and the opens site of `U.toScheme`" | **Under-specified** — the blueprint describes the endpoint (site equivalence → sheaf equivalence) but does NOT describe the prerequisite step: proving that the functors of `Opens.overEquivalence U` are `IsCocontinuous`, which is the entire substance of the iter-030 work. A prover cannot formalize C from the current blueprint sketch without discovering the IsCocontinuous infrastructure independently. |
| **P1** `lem:isIso_fromTildeΓ_basicOpen_of_quasicoherent` | Full chain: `QuasicoherentData.bind` + `Presentation.map` + C + affine identification + `isIso_fromTildeΓ_of_presentation` | **Adequate** once C is available. |
| **D** `lem:section_localization_descent` | Mayer–Vietoris equalizer + flatness of localization + per-cover tilde identifications | **Adequate** — detailed enough to guide formalization. |
| **gap1 assembly** `lem:qcoh_affine_isIso_fromTildeΓ` | One-liner: D → `isIso_fromTildeΓ_iff_isLocalizedModule_restrict` | **Adequate**. |

### (c) Hint precision

**Imprecise (major):** The `\lean{}` pin at `thm:grassmannian_representable` names
`AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Grassmannian.representable`, but the blueprint's own NOTE says this pin
"points at a declaration that under-delivers the prose statement; it should be strengthened or split
into a separate skeleton label." The pin is technically correct (the name exists) but the prose
statement is substantially stronger than the Lean statement. The blueprint should either (a) weaken
the prose to match the skeleton, or (b) add a skeleton-label to mark the current statement as
a partial formalization.

**Absent (minor):** No `\lean{}` pins exist for the 6 OverSiteSheafEquivalence declarations.
Since these are project-local infra, this is expected, but the absence means the blueprint DAG
does not capture their role as prerequisites of C. A blueprint update should add at least a block
for `overEquivalence_sheafCongr` and wire it into `lem:over_restrict_iso`'s `\uses` chain.

### (d) Generality

Matches need. The OverSiteSheafEquivalence section correctly states the sheaf-equivalence for an
arbitrary category `A` (not just `RingCat`), as needed by the eventual `overRestrictIso` construction.

### Recommended chapter-side actions

1. **(Major) C under-specification:** Add a blueprint block for `overEquivalence_sheafCongr`
   (and optionally the two `IsCocontinuous` instances) explaining that the Mathlib TODO for
   `Opens.overEquivalence` requires proving cover-lifting for both functors before
   `Equivalence.sheafCongr` can be invoked. Wire this into `lem:over_restrict_iso`'s `\uses` chain.

2. **(Major) Grassmannian.representable pin:** The `\lean{}` pin at `thm:grassmannian_representable`
   should be split — add a `\lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Grassmannian.representable}` label on a
   weaker skeleton-only statement block, and keep the stronger prose statement as an unformalized
   theorem awaiting upgrade (no `\leanok`).

3. **(Minor) Dependency edges for gap1 cone:** Add `overEquivalence_sheafCongr` (and the five
   cocontinuity/continuity instances) as project-local declaration blocks under C's

points at an under-delivering declaration.
